steps.title: Identify the zeros from x-intercepts
steps.content: The graph passes through $(-3, 0)$, $(1, 0)$, and $(4, 0)$. These are the x-intercepts, meaning $f(-3) = 0$, $f(1) = 0$, and $f(4) = 0$.

steps.title: Apply Factor Theorem to match the choices
steps.content: By the Factor Theorem: if $f(a) = 0$, then $(x - a)$ is a factor. Our zeros give us factors $(x + 3)$, $(x - 1)$, and $(x - 4)$. Looking at the answer choices, only $(x - 1)$ appears as a factor. That matches choice C — C is correct.
